Great list, what if you have an inground pool, do you or when should you drain it and like how much?

we have taken ours down a couple of inches today. We are in Port Saint Lucie & it is already raining & not supposed to stop ahead of the storm. We do this NOT for worry of flooding (there is a house long drain between pool & house), but so that the water will stay in the level to keep the filter running up to losing electricity if that happens. Water either too deep or too shallow will cause it not to be at the level to run into the opening with the flap where the basket is. We will monitor & adjust again in a couple of days. Other than that, leave inground pool alone.
do not drain your pool!

Why is there always a concern that there will not be water? Thanks
one reason is that if the area has no power for extended periods of time, the water treatment plant is not able to supply non contaminated water

without water you can’t do basic things like brush teeth, flush toilets, shower, etc and if it lasts for weeks (which it has for us in the cape) you will get feeling pretty gross and things will get really unsanitary quick
you don’t want to risk drinking contaminated water as they can’t test till after storm
